title: Fix the Simple Array Min Finder

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an array of integers as input and returns the minimum value in the array. If the array is empty, the function should return null. The function should work for both positive and negative integers.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a string representing an array of integers, space-separated.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a string representing the minimum value in the array, or null if the array is empty.
statement.notes: Do not use built-in array methods like Math.min() or sort().

examples.explanation: The minimum value in the array is 10.
The minimum value in the array is -5.
The array is empty, so the function returns null.

hints.text: Start by splitting the input string into an array of integers.

editorial.title: Editorial for Simple Array Min Finder
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over the array and keep track of the minimum value seen so far.
editorial.bruteForce: The function could use a nested loop to compare each pair of elements, but this would be inefficient.
editorial.optimalSolution: The function should use a single loop to iterate over the array and keep track of the minimum value.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function returns the correct minimum value for all test cases.
commonMistakes: Not handling the empty array case
